column1: Here is a complete table listing Sudhana's 53 teachers (lamp-lights) from the Gandavyūha Sūtra, each regarded as a beacon of light, alongside their connection to the Huayan (Huayan = Flower Ornament) stages of the Bodhisattva path. The Huayan school interprets Sudhana’s pilgrimage as progressing through the bodhisattva stages, though not every teacher is mapped one-to-one to a specific stage. The journey as a whole is seen as an allegory for the progression through the 52 stages (plus final enlightenment) of the bodhisattva path578.
The list above is based on the translation and summary provided by Jeff Watt, drawing from Thomas Cleary’s work8.

The Huayan (Avatamsaka) school understands Sudhana’s pilgrimage as a progressive journey through the 52 bodhisattva stages: 10 Faiths, 10 Dwellings, 10 Practices, 10 Transferences, 10 Bhūmis, Equal Enlightenment, and Wonderful Enlightenment57.

Not every teacher is mapped to a single stage; rather, the journey as a whole is the allegory for the path, and the last teachers (Maitreya, Manjushri, Samantabhadra) represent the culmination of the path578.
This table provides a comprehensive overview of Sudhana’s 53 teachers as lamp-lights in the Gandavyūha Sūtra and their symbolic connection to the Huayan stages of enlightenment.
